title: Unbelievably Rich German Challots Wrap
description: A rich and chewy German dish featuring challots, ssam-wrap, and low-carb-friendly ingredients, perfect for a sick day. This dish is a twist on traditional German cuisine with a Korean-inspired wrap.
instructions: Preheat a large skillet over medium heat and add the sliced pork sausage.
Cook the sausage until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped challots to the skillet and cook until they are softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the sauerkraut, caraway seeds, salt, and pepper to the skillet and stir to combine.
Cook for an additional 2 minutes, until the sauerkraut is heated through.
Warm the ssam wraps by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20 seconds.
Assemble the wraps by spooning the sausage and challot mixture onto the center of each wrap.
Top each wrap with shredded cheese and a drizzle of melted butter.
Fold the bottom edge of each wrap up over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll the wrap up tightly.
